summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a special meeting and public hearing for the proposed 2023 tax budget. The administrator presented a preliminary budget overview noting that revenue is higher than expenses across departments, with savings in the service department due to ARPA and grant money covering paving costs. The police department projects approximately $3 million in revenue against $2.9 million in expenses, and the fire/EMS department has ongoing capital payments for a fire truck and two ambulances. The board approved the 2023 tax budget for submission to the county auditor by July 15.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda passed unanimously
Motion to open the public hearing for 2023 tax budget passed unanimously
Motion to close the public hearing for 2023 tax budget passed unanimously
Motion to approve the proposed 2023 tax budget passed unanimously
Motion to adjourn at 6:14 PM passed
action_items: Administrator to work with Fiscal Officer England to enter the 2023 tax budget into UAN and submit it to the county auditor by July 15
Administration to conduct a deeper budget review closer to December with real numbers
Public hearing on the July tax budget to be advertised on the township website and in the newspaper per ORC requirements
key_amounts: $662,000 ending unencumbered balance (general fund), compared to $656,000 last year
$980,000 projected service department revenue next year
Over $3 million projected police department revenue
$893,000 projected police department ending unencumbered balance
Fire engine paid off in 2027; two ambulances paid off in 2024
notable_topics: 2023 preliminary tax budget presentation and public hearing
Service department savings due to ARPA and grant money covering paving costs
Police department budget and vehicle purchases planned for next year
Fire/EMS capital payments (fire truck Station 19 and two ambulances)
Fire and EMS levy renewal discussion anticipated for 2024
Zoning revenue exceeding full-year projections already
Upcoming concerns about property/casualty insurance, workers comp, and contract renewals
notes: Meeting was held earlier than usual due to scheduling conflicts. The administrator emphasized that the July tax budget is essentially a cut-and-paste of the previous year's budget since actual revenue and expense figures are not yet known this early. A regular meeting was scheduled to follow this special meeting, with paving discussions on the agenda. No members of the public were present to comment during the public hearing.
title: Spc. Mtg 6.28.22 July Tax Budget.mp4
